violations.text: Various food items in the walk-in cooler was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of by person in charge. Ice machine taken out of service until it can be washed/rinsed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

Various cream cheese and milk was observed held at 48-52 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit. The ambient of the prep unit was observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, foods were in the prep unit for 3 hours. Foods moved to walk-in cooler to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

Various food items in the walk-in cooler was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the ceiling vents and the fan in the front service area was observed with an accumulation of dust and grease. The vents shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent the possible contamination of food. New Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH Center for Food Protection. PIC given CFSM application at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Person in charge voluntarily disposed of ice at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

Cream cheese was observed held at 51 degrees Fahrenheit in the flip top prep unit. Cream cheese was observed to be double panned. Double panning foods in the flip top prep unit will prohibit proper airflow. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, food was placed in the unit under 4 hours prior to inspection. Food moved to walk-in to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024
